differential_diagnoses: Occult primary malignancy (likely pancreatic or lung)
Disseminated intravascular coagulation secondary to malignancy
next_steps: Perform bone marrow aspiration and biopsy with flow cytometry and cytogenetics
Obtain ADAMTS13 activity level to definitively rule out TTP
Calculate HScore to evaluate for secondary hemophagocytic lymphohistiocytosis
Assess serum soluble IL-2 receptor and triglyceride levels
Order full body PET-CT scan to identify the primary malignant focus
Manage acute pancreatitis with supportive care and evaluate for potential underlying pancreatic malignancy
Proceed with early goals-of-care discussions with the patient and family

differential_diagnoses: Secondary hemophagocytic lymphohistiocytosis (sHLH) due to malignancy
Occult pancreatic adenocarcinoma with ductal obstruction and metastasis
Acute myeloid leukemia (AML) with myelodysplastic-related changes
next_steps: Perform bone marrow aspiration and biopsy with flow cytometry, cytogenetics, and cultures
Calculate HScore and obtain sIL-2 receptor, triglycerides, and fibrinogen to evaluate for HLH
Perform pancreatic-protocol MRI to evaluate the pancreatic tail lesion and ductal anatomy
Re-evaluate peripheral blood smear with an expert hematopathologist
Initiate supportive care for pancreatitis and address potential nutritional/metabolic imbalances
Discuss clinical status and goals-of-care with the patient and surrogate
